ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'sandwich' class (FOODON_00002473); no specific beef-and-mushroom melt sub-class exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers sandwiches and filled rolls as a category; no specific beef-mushroom melt code exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for beef and mushroom melt sandwich identified in publicly available SNOMED CT browser.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 6-inch Subway-style beef and mushroom melt sandwich weighing approximately 250 g, comprising a bread roll, roast beef, sautéed mushrooms, melted cheese, and assorted vegetables with condiments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Submarine sandwich with meat and cheese on white bread (SR Legacy ID 21299) and component-level data for beef, mushrooms, and cheese
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Subway Beef and Mushroom Melt entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods with full nutrient panel. Values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for submarine sandwich with meat and cheese (ID 21299, ~220 kcal/100g, protein ~12g, fat ~9g, carbohydrate ~22g) and USDA component data for roast beef, mushrooms, and American cheese. Input values (energy 180 kcal, protein 11.2g, fat 8.0g, carbohydrate 15.2g) are plausible for a lower-calorie 6-inch variant with moderate cheese and lean beef. Values retained with medium confidence and documented adaptation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamins A/B-group, vitamin D/E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water set to -1 as not reported in the input and not reliably derivable for this composite dish without exact recipe weights.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Subway Beef and Mushroom Melt. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on analogous white-bread-based mixed sandwich entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report GI values of 45–60 for mixed meat-and-cheese sandwiches on white or wheat bread. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (53) × available carbohydrate per serving (38.0 g for 250 g portion) / 100 = 20.1. GI 53 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database classification (low <55, medium 55–69, high ≥70).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 380 mg per 100 g; a full 250 g serving delivers approximately 950 mg sodium, representing 41–63% of the recommended daily limit of 1500–2300 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a 6-inch sandwich, request no added sauces, and choose wholegrain bread to partially offset sodium load with additional potassium and fibre.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 15.2 g per 100 g (38 g per 250 g serving) with low dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g) yields a glycemic load of approximately 20 per serving, which can elevate postprandial blood glucose, particularly with white Italian bread.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should choose wholegrain or honey oat bread to lower the glycemic index, reduce or omit sweet sauces, and pair the sandwich with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.2 g per 100 g (8.0 g per 250 g serving) from cheese and beef, combined with 0.2 g trans fat per 100 g and high sodium (380 mg/100 g), collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit cheese quantity, request no mayonnaise, choose lean beef portions, and select wholegrain bread to reduce saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium intake per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Roast beef contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which can amplify inflammatory cytokine production; sodium at 380 mg/100 g may exacerbate tissue oedema in active RA.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting beef with chicken breast to reduce arachidonic acid load, and minimise added sauces to limit sodium-driven fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Beef and Mushroom Melt Sandwich is a toasted submarine-style sandwich sold at Subway outlets in Singapore, typically available as a 6-inch or footlong option. It consists of thinly sliced roast beef, sautéed or canned mushrooms, and melted processed cheese layered on a soft bread roll (white Italian, wholegrain, or honey oat), topped with fresh vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and capsicum, and finished with condiments including mayonnaise or other sauces. The sandwich is assembled cold and then toasted in a conveyor oven, producing a warm, slightly crispy exterior with a soft, chewy interior. The flavour profile is savoury and umami-rich, with the mushrooms contributing earthiness, the beef providing a mild meaty depth, and the melted cheese adding creaminess. Per 100 g, the sandwich provides approximately 180 kcal, 11.2 g protein, 8.0 g fat (3.2 g saturated), 15.2 g carbohydrate, 1.6 g dietary fibre, and 380 mg sodium. A standard 250 g serving delivers roughly 450 kcal and 950 mg sodium. The sodium content is a primary clinical concern for hypertensive individuals. The estimated glycemic index of 53 (low) and glycemic load of approximately 20 per serving make it a moderate choice for people with type 2 diabetes, particularly if white bread is selected. The saturated fat and trans fat content are relevant for cardiovascular disease management. The dish is certified halal and is a common lunch or dinner option in Singapore's fast-casual dining segment.

confidence_reason: Input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from GrabFood, a forbidden primary nutrient source. Nutrient values were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy submarine sandwich with meat and cheese (ID 21299) and found plausible but not exactly matched. No HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this specific Subway product was identified. Medium confidence assigned as values are defensible via USDA analogy but not confirmed by an exact approved-source entry.
notes: 1. Nutrient values sourced from input (ai_generated, GrabFood) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy submarine sandwich with meat and cheese (ID 21299). Values are plausible but represent an adapted estimate, not an exact match. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, minerals, and moisture data for this composite fast-food sandwich are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16 for this specific product. 3. GI estimated at 53 (low) from analogous mixed meat-and-cheese sandwich entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this product exists. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = 53 × 38.0 / 100 = 20.1. GI 53 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database classification (low <55, medium 55–69, high ≥70). 4. Two portions in the input record both list 250 g — the footlong and 6-inch labels appear to share the same gram weight, which is inconsistent (a footlong is typically ~400–450 g). The 250 g figure has been retained as the default serving size for the 6-inch variant; the footlong portion weight should be verified against Subway Singapore nutritional disclosures. 5. Halal dietary tag preserved from input; Subway Singapore holds MUIS halal certification. 6. FoodOn FOODON_00002473 and FoodEx2 A0FHR applied as closest_parent ontology anchors; no exact sub-class for beef-mushroom melt sandwich exists in either system. Match types corrected from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the scoring model.
